copy.keyTakeaway: Pirates beat the Astros in a slugfest, Guardians dismantled the Yankees, and tech stocks took another hit as the 10-year yield climbed. NBA Finals tip off tonight.

todaysHits.sports: Pirates 10, Astros 6. Guardians 9, Yankees 4. Brewers 8, Giants 3.
todaysHits.golf: Brian Campbell leads Memorial Tournament at even.
todaysHits.culture: Russo Bros restart MCU, Black Crowes clash with crowd, Love Island Season 8 premieres.

sportsOther: Pirates came to Houston and took what they wanted.
Guardians walked into the Bronx and left no doubt.
Brewers made it look easy against a Giants lineup that couldn't find the zone.

markets.mood: Tech got hammered across the board—NVDA down 3.6%, MSFT and DELL both down over 3%—while the broader market barely flinched, which tells you investors are rotating out of the mega-cap AI bet and into safer ground.
markets.whyBullet1: If your 401k is heavy on tech index funds, you're feeling this today; the SPY only dropped 0.7% because everything else held up, but your concentration risk just showed up in real time.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch the 10-year Treasury yield climbing 0.8% today—if that keeps running, it pressures growth stocks even more, so Friday's jobs report becomes the real test of whether this is a rotation or the start of something worse.
markets.bringUp: The 10-year is up 0.2% for the whole week but jumped 0.8% today alone, which means the big move happened in one day—that's not gradual repositioning, that's fear about something specific.

golf.headline: Campbell, Coody, Fox deadlocked at Memorial's tough Muirfield Village
golf.whyCare1: Muirfield Village is basically the PGA Tour's final exam before the playoffs — winning here means everything for momentum into the FedEx Cup.
golf.whyCare2: The course is playing brutal this week, which means whoever figures out the greens first gets a massive advantage over three guys tied at the top.
golf.watchFor: Whether Campbell can hold the lead or if Coody's consistency catches up — these two are the real story, not the packed leaderboard behind them.
golf.whatToSay: If Campbell wins this, he's basically locked into the playoffs conversation. If he doesn't, Coody just proved he belongs with the best.

f1.headline: Monaco's Here. Max Wants His Crown Back.
f1.whyCare1: Monaco separates the truly elite drivers from everyone else—one mistake ends your race, no second chances.
f1.whyCare2: Verstappen needs to close his championship gap before the summer break hits, and this narrow street circuit suits precision over raw pace.
f1.watchFor: How Verstappen qualifies. Pole position essentially wins Monaco. Everything else is just damage control.
f1.whatToSay: Monaco's the one race where you can't just muscle your way through. It's all about being smooth and not losing your mind for two hours.
